data.title: SharpLink Acquires $463M in Ether, Shares Remain 66% Lower
data.description:
SharpLink Gaming (SBET), the public firm pivoting to a crypto treasury strategy focused on Ethereum, revealed on Friday it acquired 176,271 ether ETH for nearly $463 million.The purchase makes the firm the largest ETH holder among publicly traded companies, SharpLink said in a press release.The company tapped its $1 billion at-the-market (ATM) common stock share facility for $79 million to help fund the ETH acquisition.The ETH purchase announcement came on the heels of a Thursday regulatory filing that potentially enabled investors in the firm's private placement round to sell shares, sending stock prices down 70% after market hours
source: https://www.coindesk.com/business/2025/06/13/sharplink-acquires-463m-in-ether-shares-remain-66-lower

data.title: India Investigates Cryptocurrency Tax Evasion
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Indian officials have announced that the country's tax authorities are currently investigating cases of tax evasion related to cryptocurrencies. This move comes as part of a broader effort to ensure compliance with tax regulations in the rapidly growing digital asset sector. The investigation aims to identify individuals and entities that may have failed to report cryptocurrency transactions accurately, thereby evading taxes. Authorities are focusing on ensuring that all cryptocurrency-related activities are properly documented and taxed in accordance with Indian law.

data.title: Urgency for Cryptocurrency Legislation in the U.S. Before 2026 Elections
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the opportunity to enact significant cryptocurrency legislation in the United States is rapidly diminishing, with potential long-term repercussions for the industry if missed. Marta Belcher, president of the Blockchain Association and the Filecoin Foundation, emphasized the importance of legislative action during a recent episode of Cointelegraph’s Decentralize podcast. She urged lawmakers and industry leaders to act swiftly before the 2026 election cycle potentially disrupts current progress.

Belcher highlighted the critical need for legislation to clarify key aspects of the cryptocurrency industry before the end of the year. She expressed concern over the uncertainty surrounding Congress's stance on crypto following the midterm elections, which could either support or hinder the industry. Reflecting on the previous administration's hostile environment, where the Securities and Exchange Commission under Gary Gensler and the Biden administration pursued legal actions against major tokens, Belcher noted the adverse impact on the industry, with many projects relocating abroad. She expressed hope for a shift in tides with legislation that facilitates operations within the U.S.

Currently, Congress is advancing both stablecoin and broader market structure legislation, with a potential vote on stablecoins anticipated soon. However, Belcher cautioned against relying on post-summer legislative movement due to unpredictable congressional priorities. She stressed the urgency of passing legislation this year to establish a clear compliance path, which many crypto projects are eager for. Belcher underscored the willingness of industry participants to comply, provided they understand the requirements. She also emphasized the necessity for industry-wide compromise, acknowledging that while the legislation may not be perfect, collective action is essential for progress.

data.title: SEC Withdraws Crypto Custody and DeFi Rules Introduced Under Gensler
data.description:
TL;DR The U. S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has officially withdrawn several regulatory proposals introduced under former Chair Gary Gensler, including rules targeting DeFi platforms and crypto asset custody. These measures, criticized for being overly strict, were abandoned following expert backlash and a change in leadership
source: https://crypto-economy.com/sec-withdraws-crypto-custody-and-defi-rules-introduced-under-gensler/
